title: Метаданные и сборки: таблицы, токены, манифест

cards.prompt: A metadata token is a 4-byte marker whose high byte is the table id. For a type that table is TypeDef = 0x02, so (MetadataToken >> 24) formatted X2 prints 02
cards.code: using System; class C {} int tok = typeof(C).MetadataToken; Console.WriteLine((tok >> 24).ToString("X2"));

cards.prompt: Each kind of defined entity lives in its own definition table, and the token high byte is that table id: type TypeDef 0x02, method MethodDef 0x06, field FieldDef 0x04, so it prints 02 06 04
cards.code: using System; using System.Reflection; class C { public int F; public void M(){} } Console.WriteLine($"{typeof(C).MetadataToken>>24:X2} {typeof(C).GetMethod("M").MetadataToken>>24:X2} {typeof(C).GetField("F").MetadataToken>>24:X2}");

cards.prompt: An assembly = manifest + modules + metadata; a single-file assembly has one module and the manifest lives inside it, so ManifestModule.Name is the .dll file. Prints 1 System.Private.CoreLib.dll
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var a = typeof(StringBuilder).Assembly; Console.WriteLine($"{a.GetModules().Length} {a.ManifestModule.Name}");
